papers.title: Chromatin buffers torsional stress during transcription
papers.abstract: During eukaryotic transcription, Pol II must overcome nucleosome obstacles and, because of DNA's helical structure, must also rotate relative to DNA, generating torsional stress. However, there is limited understanding of how Pol II transcribes through nucleosomes while supercoiling DNA. Here, we determined that Pol II generates a torque of 9 pN·nm alone and 13 pN·nm with TFIIS, making it a powerful rotary motor. When Pol II encounters a nucleosome, passage becomes more efficient on a chromatin substrate than on a single-nucleosome substrate, demonstrating that chromatin significantly buffers torsional stress during transcription. Furthermore, topoisomerase supercoiling relaxation allows Pol II to transcribe through multiple nucleosomes. Our results reveal a role of chromatin beyond the more conventional view of it being just a roadblock to transcription.

papers.llm_title_bilingual: 染色质在转录过程中缓冲扭转应力 / Chromatin buffers torsional stress during transcription
papers.llm_research_question_zh: RNA聚合酶II在真核转录过程中如何在产生DNA扭转应力与超螺旋的情况下穿越核小体，以及染色质与拓扑异构酶在这一过程中的作用是什么？
llm_highlights_zh: RNA聚合酶II单独可产生9 pN·nm扭矩，加入TFIIS后增至13 pN·nm，表明其是强有力的旋转分子马达。
与单核小体底物相比，Pol II在染色质底物上通过核小体更高效，提示染色质可在转录过程中缓冲扭转应力。
拓扑异构酶介导的超螺旋松弛可促进Pol II跨越多个核小体转录，揭示染色质并非仅是转录障碍。
papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 发表于《Science》391卷6792期（2026年3月）。
papers.llm_pubmed_abstract_zh: 在真核转录过程中，RNA聚合酶II（Pol II）不仅需要克服核小体这一障碍，而且由于DNA具有螺旋结构，还必须相对于DNA发生旋转，从而产生扭转应力。然而，目前对于Pol II如何在DNA发生超螺旋化的同时穿越核小体，仍缺乏充分认识。本文发现，Pol II单独可产生9 pN·nm的扭矩，在TFIIS存在时可产生13 pN·nm的扭矩，表明其是一种强有力的旋转分子马达。当Pol II遇到核小体时，其在染色质底物上的穿越效率高于在单核小体底物上的效率，说明染色质在转录过程中可显著缓冲扭转应力。此外，拓扑异构酶介导的超螺旋松弛可使Pol II转录穿越多个核小体。研究结果揭示了染色质除传统意义上作为转录障碍之外的另一种作用。

papers.title: A genetically encoded device for transcriptome storage in mammalian cells
papers.abstract: Understanding how cells make decisions over time requires the ability to link past molecular states to future phenotypic outcomes. We present TimeVault, a genetically encoded system that records and stores transcriptomes within living mammalian cells for future readout. TimeVault leverages engineered vault particles that capture mRNA through poly(A) binding protein. We demonstrate that the transcriptome stored by TimeVaults is stable in living cells for over 7 days. TimeVault enables high-fidelity transcriptome-wide recording with minimal cellular perturbation, capturing transient stress responses and revealing gene expression changes underlying drug-naive persister states in lung cancer cells that evade EGFR inhibition. By linking past and present cellular states, TimeVault provides a powerful tool for decoding how cells respond to stress, make fate decisions, and resist therapy.

papers.llm_title_bilingual: 哺乳动物细胞中用于转录组存储的基因编码装置 / A genetically encoded device for transcriptome storage in mammalian cells
papers.llm_research_question_zh: 如何在哺乳动物活细胞中以低扰动、高保真方式记录并长期保存既往转录组状态，从而将过去的分子状态与未来的细胞表型和命运结局联系起来？
llm_highlights_zh: 提出TimeVault，一种可在哺乳动物活细胞中记录并存储转录组的基因编码系统。

papers.primary_summary_raw: Understanding how cells make decisions over time requires the ability to link past molecular states to future phenotypic outcomes. We present TimeVault, a genetically encoded system that records and stores transcriptomes within living mammalian cells for future readout. TimeVault leverages engineered vault particles that capture mRNA through poly(A) binding protein. We demonstrate that the transcriptome stored by TimeVaults is stable in living cells for over 7 days. TimeVault enables high-fidelity transcriptome-wide recording with minimal cellular perturbation, capturing transient stress responses and revealing gene expression changes underlying drug-naive persister states in lung cancer cells that evade EGFR inhibition. By linking past and present cellular states, TimeVault provides a powerful tool for decoding how cells respond to stress, make fate decisions, and resist therapy.

papers.title: fRagmentomics: an R package for integrating cell-free DNA fragment features with mutational status to support liquid biopsy interpretation
papers.summary: Abstract Summary Liquid biopsy offers a non-invasive approach to study tumor-derived genetic material circulating in plasma. Beyond genetic alterations, the fragmentomic features of cell-free DNA—such as fragment size, genomic position, and end-motifs—provide valuable insights into the biological and clinical context of DNA release. fRagmentomics is a user-friendly R package designed to characterize cfDNA fragments overlapping one or multiple small mutations of any type, starting from an aligned sequencing file (BAM). It supports multiple mutation input formats, accommodates one-based and zero-based genomic conventions, resolves mutation representation ambiguities, and accepts any reference file in FASTA format. For each fragment overlapping a mutation of interest, fRagmentomics outputs fragment-level features including its fragment size, end-motifs, and mutational status, along with additional fragment-level or read-level information. The package implements an indel-aware and optionally soft-clip-preserving fragment size computation that improves accuracy over conventional size estimates based solely on aligned positions. Availability and implementation fRagmentomics is licensed under GNU General Public License v3.0 and available at https://github.com/ElsaB-Lab/fRagmentomics and https://anaconda.org/elsab-lab/r-fragmentomics , with documentation and a tutorial
papers.llm_title_zh: fRagmentomics：用于整合细胞游离DNA片段特征与突变状态以支持液体活检解释的 R 软件包
papers.llm_title_bilingual: fRagmentomics：用于整合细胞游离DNA片段特征与突变状态以支持液体活检解释的 R 软件包 / fRagmentomics: an R package for integrating cell-free DNA fragment features with mutational status to support liquid biopsy interpretation
papers.llm_research_question_zh: 如何开发一款可从比对测序数据中整合 cfDNA 片段特征与小突变状态的分析工具，以支持液体活检中肿瘤来源 DNA 的更准确解释？
llm_highlights_zh: fRagmentomics 是一款面向液体活检解释的易用型 R 软件包，可将 cfDNA 片段组学特征与突变状态进行整合分析。
该工具从比对后的 BAM 文件出发，支持多种小突变输入格式、不同基因组坐标体系，并可处理突变表示歧义。
软件可输出突变重叠片段的长度、末端基序及突变状态等片段级信息，并提供考虑插入缺失且可保留软剪切的片段长度计算。
papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 液体活检为研究血浆中循环的肿瘤来源遗传物质提供了无创途径。除遗传变异外，细胞游离 DNA（cfDNA）的片段组学特征，如片段长度、基因组位置和末端基序，也可为 DNA 释放的生物学与临床背景提供重要信息。fRagmentomics 是一款易于使用的 R 软件包，旨在从比对后的测序文件（BAM）出发，对与一个或多个任意类型小突变重叠的 cfDNA 片段进行表征。该软件支持多种突变输入格式，兼容从 1 开始和从 0 开始的基因组坐标体系，可解析突变表示歧义，并接受任意 FASTA 格式参考文件。对于每条与目标突变重叠的片段，fRagmentomics 可输出片段长度、末端基序和突变状态等片段级特征，以及其他附加的片段级或读段级信息。该软件实现了可识别插入缺失、并可选择保留软剪切信息的片段长度计算方法，相较于仅依据比对位置的传统长度估计具有更高准确性。软件采用 GNU General Public License v3.0 许可，可通过 GitHub 与 Anaconda 获取，并提供文档和教程。

papers.title: DeepLMI: Deep Feature Mining with a Globally Enhanced Graph Convolutional Network for Robust lncRNA–miRNA Interaction Prediction
papers.summary: Abstract Motivation Interactions between long non-coding RNAs (lncRNAs) and microRNAs (miRNAs) play pivotal roles in gene regulation and disease progression, notably through mechanisms such as competitive miRNA sponging. Accurate identification of lncRNA–miRNA interactions is therefore essential for understanding disease mechanisms and discovering therapeutic targets. However, current knowledge is largely derived from labor-intensive and costly biological experiments, underscoring the need for reliable computational approaches. Results We propose DeepLMI, a novel deep learning framework for lncRNA–miRNA interaction prediction that integrates deep feature mining with a globally enhanced graph convolutional network. To effectively capture the distinct properties of lncRNAs and miRNAs, DeepLMI employs specialized feature extraction modules: for lncRNAs, we combine sequence pre-training with self-attention mechanisms to learn multi-scale semantic representations; for miRNAs, we fuse heterogeneous features through a graph convolutional encoder. To further address the sparsity and structural complexity of known RNA interaction networks, we design a Global-Enhanced Graph Convolutional Network (GE-GCN) that jointly models local neighborhood information and global topological signals. The embeddings learned for lncRNAs and miRNAs are then integrated to infer interaction probabilities. Extensive experiments across multiple datasets and evaluation settings demonstrate that DeepLMI consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods and exhibits strong robustness, highlighting its potential as a valuable tool for RNA interaction analysis and disease research. Availability The codes and data are publicly available at https://github.com/Hhhzj-7/DeepLMI
papers.llm_title_zh: 基于全局增强图卷积网络与深度特征挖掘的稳健lncRNA–miRNA相互作用预测：DeepLMI
papers.llm_title_bilingual: 基于全局增强图卷积网络与深度特征挖掘的稳健lncRNA–miRNA相互作用预测：DeepLMI / DeepLMI: Deep Feature Mining with a Globally Enhanced Graph Convolutional Network for Robust lncRNA–miRNA Interaction Prediction
papers.llm_research_question_zh: 如何构建一种能够同时刻画lncRNA与miRNA异质特征，并有效利用稀疏且结构复杂的RNA相互作用网络局部与全局拓扑信息的计算模型，以更稳健、准确地预测lncRNA–miRNA相互作用？
llm_highlights_zh: 提出DeepLMI框架，将深度特征挖掘与全局增强图卷积网络结合用于lncRNA–miRNA相互作用预测。
针对lncRNA与miRNA分别设计特征提取模块，并通过GE-GCN联合建模局部邻域与全局拓扑信息。
在多个数据集和评估设置中表现优于现有先进方法，显示出较强稳健性与应用潜力。
papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 长链非编码RNA（lncRNA）与微小RNA（miRNA）之间的相互作用在基因调控和疾病进展中具有关键作用，如竞争性miRNA海绵机制。由于现有相关知识主要依赖耗时且成本较高的生物学实验，开发可靠的计算预测方法具有重要意义。该研究提出DeepLMI，一种用于lncRNA–miRNA相互作用预测的深度学习框架，融合深度特征挖掘与全局增强图卷积网络。为刻画lncRNA与miRNA的不同特性，模型分别采用专门的特征提取模块：对lncRNA，结合序列预训练与自注意力机制以学习多尺度语义表征；对miRNA，通过图卷积编码器融合异质特征。为进一步应对已知RNA相互作用网络的稀疏性和结构复杂性，研究设计了全局增强图卷积网络（GE-GCN），联合建模局部邻域信息与全局拓扑信号。随后整合lncRNA和miRNA的嵌入表示以推断相互作用概率。多数据集和多种评估设置下的大量实验表明，DeepLMI持续优于现有最先进方法，并表现出良好的稳健性，提示其在RNA相互作用分析和疾病研究中具有应用价值。代码和数据已公开。

all_papers.title: Chromatin buffers torsional stress during transcription
all_papers.abstract: During eukaryotic transcription, Pol II must overcome nucleosome obstacles and, because of DNA's helical structure, must also rotate relative to DNA, generating torsional stress. However, there is limited understanding of how Pol II transcribes through nucleosomes while supercoiling DNA. Here, we determined that Pol II generates a torque of 9 pN·nm alone and 13 pN·nm with TFIIS, making it a powerful rotary motor. When Pol II encounters a nucleosome, passage becomes more efficient on a chromatin substrate than on a single-nucleosome substrate, demonstrating that chromatin significantly buffers torsional stress during transcription. Furthermore, topoisomerase supercoiling relaxation allows Pol II to transcribe through multiple nucleosomes. Our results reveal a role of chromatin beyond the more conventional view of it being just a roadblock to transcription.

all_papers.title: A genetically encoded device for transcriptome storage in mammalian cells
all_papers.abstract: Understanding how cells make decisions over time requires the ability to link past molecular states to future phenotypic outcomes. We present TimeVault, a genetically encoded system that records and stores transcriptomes within living mammalian cells for future readout. TimeVault leverages engineered vault particles that capture mRNA through poly(A) binding protein. We demonstrate that the transcriptome stored by TimeVaults is stable in living cells for over 7 days. TimeVault enables high-fidelity transcriptome-wide recording with minimal cellular perturbation, capturing transient stress responses and revealing gene expression changes underlying drug-naive persister states in lung cancer cells that evade EGFR inhibition. By linking past and present cellular states, TimeVault provides a powerful tool for decoding how cells respond to stress, make fate decisions, and resist therapy.

all_papers.primary_summary_zh: 理解细胞如何随时间作出决策，需要能够将既往分子状态与未来表型结局联系起来。本文提出TimeVault，这是一种基因编码系统，可在活体哺乳动物细胞内记录并存储转录组，以供后续读取。TimeVault利用工程化vault颗粒通过多聚(A)结合蛋白捕获mRNA。作者证明，TimeVault所存储的转录组在活细胞内可稳定保存超过7天。TimeVault能够以较小的细胞扰动实现高保真、全转录组范围的记录，既可捕捉短暂的应激反应，也可揭示肺癌细胞中逃避EGFR抑制的药物初始耐受持续存活状态背后的基因表达变化。通过连接细胞过去与当前的状态，TimeVault为解析细胞如何响应应激、作出命运决定以及产生治疗耐受提供了有力工具。

all_papers.title: fRagmentomics: an R package for integrating cell-free DNA fragment features with mutational status to support liquid biopsy interpretation
all_papers.summary: Abstract Summary Liquid biopsy offers a non-invasive approach to study tumor-derived genetic material circulating in plasma. Beyond genetic alterations, the fragmentomic features of cell-free DNA—such as fragment size, genomic position, and end-motifs—provide valuable insights into the biological and clinical context of DNA release. fRagmentomics is a user-friendly R package designed to characterize cfDNA fragments overlapping one or multiple small mutations of any type, starting from an aligned sequencing file (BAM). It supports multiple mutation input formats, accommodates one-based and zero-based genomic conventions, resolves mutation representation ambiguities, and accepts any reference file in FASTA format. For each fragment overlapping a mutation of interest, fRagmentomics outputs fragment-level features including its fragment size, end-motifs, and mutational status, along with additional fragment-level or read-level information. The package implements an indel-aware and optionally soft-clip-preserving fragment size computation that improves accuracy over conventional size estimates based solely on aligned positions. Availability and implementation fRagmentomics is licensed under GNU General Public License v3.0 and available at https://github.com/ElsaB-Lab/fRagmentomics and https://anaconda.org/elsab-lab/r-fragmentomics , with documentation and a tutorial
all_papers.llm_title_zh: fRagmentomics：用于整合细胞游离DNA片段特征与突变状态以支持液体活检解释的 R 软件包
all_papers.llm_title_bilingual: fRagmentomics：用于整合细胞游离DNA片段特征与突变状态以支持液体活检解释的 R 软件包 / fRagmentomics: an R package for integrating cell-free DNA fragment features with mutational status to support liquid biopsy interpretation
all_papers.llm_research_question_zh: 如何开发一款可从比对测序数据中整合 cfDNA 片段特征与小突变状态的分析工具，以支持液体活检中肿瘤来源 DNA 的更准确解释？
llm_highlights_zh: fRagmentomics 是一款面向液体活检解释的易用型 R 软件包，可将 cfDNA 片段组学特征与突变状态进行整合分析。
该工具从比对后的 BAM 文件出发，支持多种小突变输入格式、不同基因组坐标体系，并可处理突变表示歧义。
软件可输出突变重叠片段的长度、末端基序及突变状态等片段级信息，并提供考虑插入缺失且可保留软剪切的片段长度计算。
all_papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 液体活检为研究血浆中循环的肿瘤来源遗传物质提供了无创途径。除遗传变异外，细胞游离 DNA（cfDNA）的片段组学特征，如片段长度、基因组位置和末端基序，也可为 DNA 释放的生物学与临床背景提供重要信息。fRagmentomics 是一款易于使用的 R 软件包，旨在从比对后的测序文件（BAM）出发，对与一个或多个任意类型小突变重叠的 cfDNA 片段进行表征。该软件支持多种突变输入格式，兼容从 1 开始和从 0 开始的基因组坐标体系，可解析突变表示歧义，并接受任意 FASTA 格式参考文件。对于每条与目标突变重叠的片段，fRagmentomics 可输出片段长度、末端基序和突变状态等片段级特征，以及其他附加的片段级或读段级信息。该软件实现了可识别插入缺失、并可选择保留软剪切信息的片段长度计算方法，相较于仅依据比对位置的传统长度估计具有更高准确性。软件采用 GNU General Public License v3.0 许可，可通过 GitHub 与 Anaconda 获取，并提供文档和教程。

all_papers.title: DeepLMI: Deep Feature Mining with a Globally Enhanced Graph Convolutional Network for Robust lncRNA–miRNA Interaction Prediction
all_papers.summary: Abstract Motivation Interactions between long non-coding RNAs (lncRNAs) and microRNAs (miRNAs) play pivotal roles in gene regulation and disease progression, notably through mechanisms such as competitive miRNA sponging. Accurate identification of lncRNA–miRNA interactions is therefore essential for understanding disease mechanisms and discovering therapeutic targets. However, current knowledge is largely derived from labor-intensive and costly biological experiments, underscoring the need for reliable computational approaches. Results We propose DeepLMI, a novel deep learning framework for lncRNA–miRNA interaction prediction that integrates deep feature mining with a globally enhanced graph convolutional network. To effectively capture the distinct properties of lncRNAs and miRNAs, DeepLMI employs specialized feature extraction modules: for lncRNAs, we combine sequence pre-training with self-attention mechanisms to learn multi-scale semantic representations; for miRNAs, we fuse heterogeneous features through a graph convolutional encoder. To further address the sparsity and structural complexity of known RNA interaction networks, we design a Global-Enhanced Graph Convolutional Network (GE-GCN) that jointly models local neighborhood information and global topological signals. The embeddings learned for lncRNAs and miRNAs are then integrated to infer interaction probabilities. Extensive experiments across multiple datasets and evaluation settings demonstrate that DeepLMI consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods and exhibits strong robustness, highlighting its potential as a valuable tool for RNA interaction analysis and disease research. Availability The codes and data are publicly available at https://github.com/Hhhzj-7/DeepLMI
all_papers.llm_title_zh: 基于全局增强图卷积网络与深度特征挖掘的稳健lncRNA–miRNA相互作用预测：DeepLMI
all_papers.llm_title_bilingual: 基于全局增强图卷积网络与深度特征挖掘的稳健lncRNA–miRNA相互作用预测：DeepLMI / DeepLMI: Deep Feature Mining with a Globally Enhanced Graph Convolutional Network for Robust lncRNA–miRNA Interaction Prediction
all_papers.llm_research_question_zh: 如何构建一种能够同时刻画lncRNA与miRNA异质特征，并有效利用稀疏且结构复杂的RNA相互作用网络局部与全局拓扑信息的计算模型，以更稳健、准确地预测lncRNA–miRNA相互作用？
llm_highlights_zh: 提出DeepLMI框架，将深度特征挖掘与全局增强图卷积网络结合用于lncRNA–miRNA相互作用预测。
针对lncRNA与miRNA分别设计特征提取模块，并通过GE-GCN联合建模局部邻域与全局拓扑信息。
在多个数据集和评估设置中表现优于现有先进方法，显示出较强稳健性与应用潜力。
all_papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 长链非编码RNA（lncRNA）与微小RNA（miRNA）之间的相互作用在基因调控和疾病进展中具有关键作用，如竞争性miRNA海绵机制。由于现有相关知识主要依赖耗时且成本较高的生物学实验，开发可靠的计算预测方法具有重要意义。该研究提出DeepLMI，一种用于lncRNA–miRNA相互作用预测的深度学习框架，融合深度特征挖掘与全局增强图卷积网络。为刻画lncRNA与miRNA的不同特性，模型分别采用专门的特征提取模块：对lncRNA，结合序列预训练与自注意力机制以学习多尺度语义表征；对miRNA，通过图卷积编码器融合异质特征。为进一步应对已知RNA相互作用网络的稀疏性和结构复杂性，研究设计了全局增强图卷积网络（GE-GCN），联合建模局部邻域信息与全局拓扑信号。随后整合lncRNA和miRNA的嵌入表示以推断相互作用概率。多数据集和多种评估设置下的大量实验表明，DeepLMI持续优于现有最先进方法，并表现出良好的稳健性，提示其在RNA相互作用分析和疾病研究中具有应用价值。代码和数据已公开。
